title: Fat Man And Little Boy

jokes.text: Miss Lucy rhyme with deliberate near-profanity fake-outs ('behind' instead of 'butt', cutting off before curse words)
jokes.setup: Traditional children's rhyme setup with innocent expectations
jokes.punchline: Multiple fake-outs where the rhyme appears to lead to profanity but diverts to innocent words
jokes.explanation: Classic children's humor with skillful misdirection, building tension with multiple fake-outs

jokes.text: Teacher interrupts the rhyme with 'Spitballs!' - perfect comedic timing
jokes.setup: Children reciting innocent-sounding rhyme
jokes.punchline: Teacher's sharp interruption reveals she knows exactly what they're doing
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - one word that cuts through the pretense

jokes.text: I'm using your unfinished novel (for spitballs)
jokes.setup: Teacher catching student with spitball materials
jokes.punchline: The ammunition is the teacher's personal creative work
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment - Bart's casual cruelty paired with teacher's failed ambitions

jokes.text: Bart claims he's bleeding but has no cut - turns out it's from a loose tooth
jokes.setup: Bart claims injury to avoid trouble
jokes.punchline: The blood is real but from an unrelated, innocent source
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection setup that drives the plot forward

jokes.text: The tooth fairy pays triple for the last baby tooth / Then let's hurry up and rip it out of my head
jokes.setup: Learning about monetary incentive for tooth
jokes.punchline: Immediate turn to violent self-harm for money
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation typical of The Simpsons - innocent childhood concept turned mercenary

jokes.text: Skinner offering his Merkur car filled with Burger King cups and wrappers as replacement
jokes.setup: Formal offer to replace damaged vehicle
jokes.punchline: The replacement is obviously unsuitable and reveals Skinner's pathetic lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner characterization - formal language masking pathetic reality

jokes.text: Potato masher's stuck in the small spoon slot. What kind of madman would do that?
jokes.setup: Minor kitchen organization problem
jokes.punchline: Dramatically disproportionate reaction calling someone a 'madman'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy - treating tiny inconvenience as grave moral failing

jokes.text: God, please give your daughter the Tooth Fairy the strength to carry my cash and the integrity not to dip her wand in the till
jokes.setup: Child's prayer about the tooth fairy
jokes.punchline: Treating mythical figure like potentially corrupt employee
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist prayer that treats childhood mythology through adult business cynicism

jokes.text: The Tooth Fairy's made a donation in my name to the United Way?! That gossamer witch!
jokes.setup: Expecting cash payment from tooth fairy
jokes.punchline: Getting a charitable donation instead, plus colorful insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with memorable phrase 'gossamer witch' - adult frustration in child's voice

jokes.text: In the right light, you're starting to get your own muzzle / I thought it was chocolate milk
jokes.setup: Homer noting Bart's growing facial hair
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent misinterpretation of what he's seeing
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment mixing Homer's bluntness with Bart's innocence about puberty

jokes.text: I knew I was an adult the day the judge said, 'We're trying you as an adult.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about when you know you're grown up
jokes.punchline: Criminal court proceedings as coming-of-age marker
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - perfect Nelson characterization with brilliant economy and timing

jokes.text: Action figure suddenly offering disability insurance
jokes.setup: Heroic action figure adventure
jokes.punchline: Lieutenant Adventure pushing insurance products
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist turn - perfect structure going from adventure fantasy to mundane adult concerns. Shows Bart's growing up anxiety.

jokes.text: Good-bye Spirogram, Sketch-N-Etch, Ravenous Ravenous Rhinos, Duopoly, Parchoosey, Humor Putty and Sock 'Em Knock 'Em Cyborgs
jokes.setup: Bart saying goodbye to childhood toys
jokes.punchline: Parody names of real board games and toys with slight alterations
jokes.explanation: Solid parody writing with recognizable toy/game references slightly altered

jokes.text: Bart singing 'Dust in the Wind' during his toy funeral
jokes.setup: Solemn moment of childhood ending
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic musical choice for toy disposal
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist choice but relies on pop culture reference without much twist

jokes.text: Bart reciting Robert Frost poem 'Nothing Gold Can Stay' at toy funeral
jokes.setup: Formal funeral for toys
jokes.punchline: Ten-year-old quoting sophisticated poetry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's intelligence beneath the troublemaker exterior

jokes.text: Sea Captain wanting a friend who isn't a work friend but being unable to tell if people are flattering him because he's a captain
jokes.setup: Adult expressing desire for genuine friendship
jokes.punchline: His authority position makes authentic relationships impossible
jokes.explanation: Thoughtful character moment exploring loneliness of authority - surprisingly deep

jokes.text: Or I could write something that's not gay
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Bart write creatively to help with depression
jokes.punchline: Bart's immature dismissal using dated slang
jokes.explanation: Weak joke relying on casual homophobia that was already dated by 2004

jokes.text: Well, I like T-shirts with a nice joke. Like, 'Support Our Troops.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about funny T-shirt slogans
jokes.punchline: Marge thinking patriotic slogan is a joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her innocent obliviousness creating unintended political commentary

jokes.text: As if I would ever want to stop truckin'
jokes.setup: Homer praising 'Keep on Truckin'' slogan
jokes.punchline: His sincere commitment to the concept of truckin'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - taking shallow slogan completely seriously and literally

jokes.text: It would cover my boy boobs nicely
jokes.setup: Homer wanting Bart's T-shirt for various reasons
jokes.punchline: Admitting he needs it to hide his feminine chest
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual body shame delivered matter-of-factly - solid character humor

jokes.text: Homer singing 'I'm Too Sexy' while wanting a T-shirt to cover his body issues
jokes.setup: Homer expressing body insecurity
jokes.punchline: Immediately singing song about being too sexy
jokes.explanation: Good ironic juxtaposition - Homer's obliviousness to contradiction in his behavior

jokes.text: My attitude isn't for sale. What am I saying? Of course it is.
jokes.setup: Bart declaring his principles
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning them upon reflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - fake nobility immediately undercut by self-awareness

jokes.text: Let's see. 'Get bent.' Well, the only thing that could mean is 'Kneel down and pray.'
jokes.setup: Reading potentially offensive T-shirt slogan
jokes.punchline: Innocent religious interpretation of crude phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Flanders characterization - his innocence creating perfect misdirection comedy

jokes.text: Do you have a T-shirt with Calvin peeing on Hobbes? / Sorry. / Well, what do you got him peeing on?
jokes.setup: Customer wanting specific crude T-shirt design
jokes.punchline: When told it doesn't exist, asking for alternatives in same category
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of actual tacky T-shirt culture with perfect escalation

jokes.text: I can't afford to pay protection to you and Nelson
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum shaking down Bart's business
jokes.punchline: Bart treating police and school bully as equivalent protection rackets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary equating corrupt police with schoolyard bullying

jokes.text: Extra-extra-extra extra-extra... ...extra-extra large? / Here you go, Chief. 'Proud Nubian Princess.' / Score!
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum needing an extremely large shirt size
jokes.punchline: Getting a shirt with feminine slogan and being excited about it
jokes.explanation: Good Chief Wiggum moment - his obliviousness to inappropriateness

jokes.text: 'And as an adult, you'll be needing comprehensive insurance'
jokes.setup: Bart declaring his adulthood
jokes.punchline: Immediate adult responsibility sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier insurance theme. Shows how adult responsibilities immediately follow declarations of maturity.

jokes.text: It works. If blue is the color for 'unimpressed.'
jokes.setup: Vendor claiming mood lollipops reveal your mood
jokes.punchline: Buyer's dry assessment that the product doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Clean deadpan response to obvious scam product

jokes.text: And we pass the slavings on to you
jokes.setup: Krusty promoting his T-shirt manufacturing
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing use of child labor with portmanteau of 'slavery' and 'savings'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay creating accidental corporate honesty about labor practices - exceptional craft

jokes.text: Itchy, Scratchy, Poochy, Austin Powers Itchy, Itchy Poochy, Scratchbob Itchpants, Confederate Itchy, and Osama Bin Scratchy
jokes.setup: Listing character T-shirt options
jokes.punchline: List escalates from normal to problematic character mashups
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing corporate willingness to exploit any trend, even inappropriate ones

jokes.text: Bad car. Apologize to the poor little boy.
jokes.setup: Car appears to hit someone
jokes.punchline: Treating the car like a misbehaving pet
jokes.explanation: Establishes Goose's eccentric character with absurdist logic

jokes.text: This shirt is humorlarious
jokes.setup: Commenting on Bart's T-shirt
jokes.punchline: Made-up portmanteau of 'humor' and 'hilarious'
jokes.explanation: Characterizes Goose as someone who makes up words - moderate wordplay

jokes.text: '20 stores in 30 states'
jokes.setup: Goose boasting about business success
jokes.punchline: Mathematically impossible business claim
jokes.explanation: Subtle math error suggesting either stupidity or fraud. Good economy.

jokes.text: Nothing makes parents happier than when an eccentric single man takes an interest in their child
jokes.setup: Goose showing unusual interest in Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious statement about obvious red flag situation
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about parental obliviousness - Homer's complete lack of appropriate concern

jokes.text: What a delightful sprite!
jokes.setup: Goose's clearly inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Marge's completely innocent assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge characterization - her innocent vocabulary for clearly problematic situation

jokes.text: Don't wake me. I'm working / Bolshevism! Sheer Bolshevism!
jokes.setup: Employee wearing anti-work T-shirt
jokes.punchline: Burns' dramatically overblown political reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - treating mild workplace humor as communist threat

jokes.text: Remove all the fillings you got under our dental plan
jokes.setup: Burns firing employee and taking away benefits
jokes.punchline: Demanding return of already-consumed medical benefits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation of Burns' pettiness - impossible and vindictive demand

jokes.text: Assuming my health does not deteriorate... as I age
jokes.setup: Homer claiming financial independence through Bart
jokes.punchline: Obvious caveat delivered as if it's unlikely
jokes.explanation: Homer treating inevitable aging as unlikely possibility - good character moment

jokes.text: I say when it's Christmas
jokes.setup: Smithers explaining why people are insolent (it's Christmas)
jokes.punchline: Burns asserting control over calendar holidays
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns megalomania - asserting control over universal concepts with complete seriousness

jokes.text: Actually, that stopped after you left, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about lunch theft at work he no longer attends
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Homer was the lunch thief
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff revealing Homer's hypocrisy with great economy

jokes.text: I broke some bathroom fixtures
jokes.setup: Homer asking for extra money beyond the bill
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of property damage
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual destruction and assumption someone else will pay

jokes.text: I don't have an inside voice!
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to use his inside voice
jokes.punchline: Homer's loud declaration that he lacks this basic social skill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - self-defeating behavior delivered with conviction

jokes.text: All the good graves are taken
jokes.setup: Nature documentary about displaced lions
jokes.punchline: Abe treating retirement home like waiting room for death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Abe's matter-of-fact acceptance of mortality with perfect economy

jokes.text: Okay, you be the girl and I'll be the car
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer to play Malibu Stacy
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of how to play with dolls
jokes.explanation: Homer's masculine incomprehension of feminine play patterns - absurdist and character-perfect

jokes.text: Screw the market, we're going to Mexico! / It's El Flanderino. Run him down! / I can't run; I'm wearing flip flops!
jokes.setup: Homer playing with Lisa's dolls inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Doll play becoming international crime spree with practical footwear concerns
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from innocent play to violent crime fantasy with perfect absurdist details

jokes.text: 'It's El Flanderino. Run him down!' / 'I can't run; I'm wearing flip flops!'
jokes.setup: Continued doll play scenario
jokes.punchline: Murder plot defeated by impractical footwear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd scenario. 'El Flanderino' plus flip-flop excuse creates funny contrast.

jokes.text: You and science go together like Lenny and Carl. The science is Carl.
jokes.setup: Homer complimenting Lisa's science aptitude
jokes.punchline: Bizarre analogy assigning scientific properties to his drinking buddy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - creating meaningless analogy and then explaining it as if it makes sense

jokes.text: Childlike Humanoid Urban Muchacho -- Or... CHUM! / Don't hold my hand. It's creepy.
jokes.setup: Martin introducing his impressive robot project
jokes.punchline: Robot immediately rejecting Martin's affection
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - creator being rejected by his creation, plus Martin's social awkwardness

jokes.text: Sweetie, that's orphan talk
jokes.setup: Lisa saying they're supposed to do science project without parental help
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing academic integrity as something only orphans would care about
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Homer logic equating ethics with lack of family - perfectly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: Korea did it, and look at the quality of their animation
jokes.setup: Homer noting that Korea built nuclear reactors
jokes.punchline: Connecting nuclear capability to animation outsourcing
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the show's own animation being outsourced to Korea - clever self-reference

jokes.text: It certainly answered a lot of my questions about wang enhancement
jokes.setup: Homer praising the internet's information resources
jokes.punchline: Revealing his primary usage is for adult content
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual oversharing about sexual insecurity - character-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No. I will never tire of the bar scene.
jokes.setup: Website asking if he's tired of the bar scene
jokes.punchline: Homer's firm commitment to bar culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priority declaration - treating drinking as lifestyle commitment

jokes.text: Just one. It takes a Marine honor guard to fold it.
jokes.setup: Someone asking how many T-shirts Comic Book Guy has
jokes.punchline: Single shirt requiring military precision to manage due to size
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about Comic Book Guy's size with military imagery

jokes.text: In fact, thanks to my team of loopy lawyers... I never have to give you another penny
jokes.setup: Goose explaining business deal to Bart
jokes.punchline: Alliterative description of corrupt legal team
jokes.explanation: Goose's casual admission of fraud with playful alliteration - reveals his character

jokes.text: The things I saw there are what made me crazy!
jokes.setup: Goose claiming to be Green Beret veteran
jokes.punchline: Admitting the experience caused his mental instability
jokes.explanation: Dark explanation for Goose's eccentric behavior - connects his quirks to trauma

jokes.text: including that guy who sells those Blu-Blocker sunglasses people sometimes wear
jokes.setup: Homer threatening massive destruction
jokes.punchline: Specifically targeting obscure infomercial spokesperson
jokes.explanation: Random specific target in destruction threat - absurdist specificity typical of the show

jokes.text: Yo, yo, yo, keep it on a low flow
jokes.setup: Novelty toilet seat that raps
jokes.punchline: Bathroom-themed rap lyrics
jokes.explanation: Novelty item gag with toilet humor - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Hey, idiot, you're fat, and your mom is naked on the Internet. You also smell.
jokes.setup: Bully trying to provoke Bart to lose tooth
jokes.punchline: Kitchen sink approach to insults including modern internet reference
jokes.explanation: Generic bully insults with contemporary internet reference - adequate but uninspired

jokes.text: You've given me a lot to think about
jokes.setup: Receiving barrage of juvenile insults
jokes.punchline: Treating crude taunts as thoughtful feedback
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - treating insults with unwarranted seriousness and politeness

tmdb_overview: When Bart writes slogans on T-shirts, he catches the attention of Goose Gladwell, a gag-gift entrepreneur, and soon becomes a T-shirt mogul; Homer feels he no longer has a place in the family when Bart becomes the breadwinner.
